behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Correctness here depends on actual neural translation quality (fluency/adequacy), which is model-output dependent and can vary across inputs and runs. A mocked translator cannot reveal true semantic/grammatical failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model/API on a curated multilingual sentence set with expected meaning-preservation criteria; score outputs for adequacy/fluency (human rubric or accepted MT metric thresholds) and fail when coherence/meaning drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: On translation API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not return an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real HTTP status handling plus fallback wiring. Using only mocks can hide status-code mapping/transport-path bugs; real HTTP response behavior is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service with a real SQLite fallback DB and a controllable HTTP endpoint for primary translation that returns an actual 503 response. Call translation endpoint and assert: successful caller response, fallback translation returned, no propagated API error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: This is a module handoff between fallback translator logic and real SQLite reads. Pure unit tests can miss schema/query/connection issues.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase->translation rows, invoke fallback translator through its service boundary, and assert exact returned translation for matching phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans algorithm + persistence. The real failure boundary is the computation result being correctly written to storage, not just local function math.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback strings, execute quality-tracking path, verify stored metric record exists and equals expected character-level edit distance.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in background.
behaviors.justification: This checks orchestration across translator selection, response path, and async/background metric logging. It is multi-module coordination but not necessarily real external-model correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators enabled (deterministic test doubles acceptable), call translate, assert response uses primary output immediately, then assert background quality log/metric is eventually persisted without blocking the response.
